title: A new interpretation of angels
wikitext:
[]: A new interpretation of angels             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Tue Aug 12, 2014 10:00 pm This is an idea I first came across on good ol' TVTropes regarding the possible identity and origin of the Angels. Basically, the idea is that the Angels don't just represent alternate evolutionary "timelines" for humanity--what we're seeing is each species's equivalent of an Eva unit. To elaborate, the Eva units were made by humans and modeled after them, so it stands to reason that if the angels represent alternate-timeline human equivalents, then they too have done the same thing. So Gaghiel, for example, was the creation of an aquatic sapient species, Matariel was created by an insectoid species, and Arael was created by a birdlike species. In this scenario, Tabris (Kaworu) would be a representative of a species that can shapeshift and uses this technique instead of an Eva equivalent. Your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by Compiling_Autumn » Tue Aug 12, 2014 11:32 pm Seems overly complicated and lacking any evidence. If this theory did have any weight to it, it would add another layer to the show that I'm not sure I would want.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Aug 13, 2014 12:10 am Oh well. I thought it was interesting.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 13, 2014 7:25 am This is directly contradicted by the show at various points (Sandalphon being the most obvious), as well as by the nature of the Evas themselves (i.e. clones of the Seeds). It really doesn't work.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Aug 13, 2014 7:43 am Oh.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Aug 13, 2014 8:30 am Bagheera wrote: This is directly contradicted by the show at various points (Sandalphon being the most obvious), as well as by the nature of the Evas themselves (i.e. clones of the Seeds). It really doesn't work.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Aug 13, 2014 8:37 am FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Though Sandalphon’s arrival is a bit weird. Though they’re obviously not Eva-like clones, I do wonder who laid an egg in a volcano. (As bad ass as it sounds, natural birth via volcanic activity is unlikely for the Angels.)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Aug 13, 2014 10:32 am I think the important distinction here, as noted by the CI, is that the Lilin was the product of millions of years of evolution that sprouted other beings (like dolphins), whereas the Angels can simply choose their form, at least to some degree.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Aug 13, 2014 11:48 am Compiling_Autumn wrote: Seems overly complicated and lacking any evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Wed Aug 13, 2014 12:35 pm You're right. I just saw the theory and wondered what the forum thought of it. Nothing wrong with that, is there?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Wed Aug 13, 2014 12:42 pm Of course not, I proposed some pretty out there theories myself, I was just mocking the nature of us geeks to overanalyse and overthink sometimes. Didn't mean to attack you.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Thu Aug 14, 2014 3:12 pm that would imply there's some tiny little "angels" sitting inside the core piloting the big angels. But as long as you're talking about waffy ideas with no point, then sure, its interesting. I mean, if angels were just human sized other versions of humans, then whence came the giant bio-robots in the first place?

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Thu Aug 14, 2014 3:31 pm Presumably, they had their own "Eva" programs.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sat Aug 16, 2014 3:47 pm I actually really like this idea. Too bad it doesn't fit with the show's mythology.

[]:             Quote Post by El Squibbonator » Sat Aug 16, 2014 5:08 pm There's always fan fiction.
